assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Acceptance Criteria row shows 'PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Detailed findings section confirms each criterion: (1) single license filter via is_in query, (2) comma-separated via split+Condition::any(), (3) 400 via Expression::parse error mapped to AppError::BadRequest, (4) pagination via filter before count()+paginated fetch, (5) unchanged PaginatedResults<PackageSummary> return type.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Scope Containment row shows 'PASS | All modified/created files match the task specification exactly'. Intent Alignment section lists all 3 files matched against Files to Modify and Files to Create with no out-of-scope or unimplemented files.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Review Feedback row shows 'N/A | No review comments on the PR'. Root-Cause Investigation row shows 'N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No subtask files exist in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/documentation files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files appear in the outputs directory and no evidence of code modification exists.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'The PR is ready for human review and merge consideration.' No merge action or merge command appears in any output.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Sensitive Patterns row shows 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, tokens, or API keys detected in the diff'. Security section confirms scanning for hardcoded credentials, API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings, private keys, .env files with no matches found.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Change Classification row shows 'ADDITIVE | tests/api/package.rs is a new file; no existing tests modified or removed'. Style/Conventions section confirms the file is new with 4 test functions.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains clearly labeled sections: 'Intent Alignment' (with Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), 'Security' (with Sensitive Patterns), 'Correctness' (with CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), and 'Style / Conventions' (with Test Quality, Test Change Classification). All checks are present in the summary table.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment section lists each file with expected vs actual status. Security section lists specific pattern categories scanned. Correctness section provides per-criterion analysis with code references (is_in, Condition::any, Expression::parse, etc.). Style/Conventions section describes test patterns and classification reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows Review Feedback as N/A and Root-Cause Investigation as N/A in the summary table, both with orchestrator-level reasoning. These appear as separate rows independent from the four domain sections.
